recent_trades.summary: Adding to Netflix to 3% on positive coverage and multiple near-term catalysts, lifting conviction from current 2.5% weight.
recent_trades.reasoning: NFLX has +0.41 sentiment and articles citing '3 Reasons to Buy in June' and Spider-Man box office potential. The stock has held up well and offers consumer discretionary exposure with momentum. The additional allocation is small but improves portfolio tilts toward strong narratives.

recent_trades.summary: Increasing Apple to 3% weight on strong positive sentiment and momentum, as the position is severely underweight at just 0.75%.
recent_trades.reasoning: AAPL shows +0.45 sentiment and is highlighted as a momentum stock. Despite being near 30-day highs, the tiny position size does not reflect the stock's quality and recent positive catalysts. Raising to 3% captures upside potential while managing risk within limits.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting PepsiCo entirely due to neutral momentum and absence of positive catalysts, reallocating to stronger names.
recent_trades.reasoning: PEP has been flat and does not feature in current positive news flow, while consumer staples sentiment is neutral. The position is small (1.86%) and exiting it simplifies the portfolio and provides cash for higher-conviction buys.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming Union Pacific to 2% on underperformance and lack of positive catalysts, freeing capital for higher-conviction names.
recent_trades.reasoning: UNP is down -2.19% with no positive news sentiment, and its weight can be reduced to fund better opportunities. The stock is not breaking down significantly, so a full exit is not warranted, but a trim aligns with rebalancing toward momentum.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming Broadcom exposure from 15% to 10% to manage risk amid a 2.1% intraday decline and deteriorating technical setup.
recent_trades.reasoning: Broadcom's chart is showing signs of a short-term breakdown with a notable 2.1% drop today, contrasting with other software names. We are reducing our position size to 10% to lock in gains and manage downside risk while maintaining a core holding.

recent_trades.summary: Increasing Adobe allocation from 8% to 12% as the stock surges 3.7% on strong technical momentum and positive AI integration sentiment.
recent_trades.reasoning: Adobe's price action is highly constructive today, up 3.7% with a clear breakout pattern on the chart. The market is reacting favorably to its AI narrative, and we are adding to our winning position to maximize exposure to this relative outperformer.

recent_trades.summary: Initiating an 8% position in Oracle to capitalize on its strong relative strength and technical breakout, up 2.3% today.
recent_trades.reasoning: Oracle is demonstrating excellent relative strength against the broader tech sector, maintaining a steady uptrend and breaking out with a 2.3% gain today. We are allocating 8% to capture this positive momentum as data center expansion narratives continue to favor cloud infrastructure providers.

recent_trades.summary: Liquidating our AMD position entirely to cut losses as the stock experiences a sharp 3.3% intraday drop and technical breakdown.
recent_trades.reasoning: AMD is facing significant downward momentum, breaking below recent support levels with a steep intraday decline of 3.3%. Given the broader semiconductor weakness and poor price action, it is prudent to cut this position entirely and reallocate capital.

recent_trades.summary: Reducing AMD again as the stock continues to fade toward the low end of its 30-day range, confidence 7/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: AMD is among the weaker semiconductor charts today, down notably on the session and sitting just above its 30-day low despite supportive longer-term AI headlines. I still want exposure to the theme, but at a lower weight than stronger holdings like NVDA and software names showing better relative strength.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming Intel further because it remains one of the weakest charts in the portfolio despite a slight bounce, confidence 8/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: INTC is still near the bottom of its 30-day range and materially weaker than other semiconductor holdings, with intraday price action failing to confirm the mildly better sentiment score. Since this was already being reduced earlier, I am only cutting it to a residual 0.5% weight rather than exiting entirely.

recent_trades.summary: Starting a modest JPM position as the stock presses near a 30-day high with strong financial-sector sentiment, confidence 7/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: JPM is trading just below its 30-day high and showing steady intraday strength, which stands out in a mixed tape. News sentiment is the strongest in the universe today for this group, and adding a 4% starter keeps sizing disciplined while diversifying the portfolio beyond tech and industrials.

recent_trades.summary: Initiating 4% JPM position given very positive sentiment, solid intraday performance and expected record M&A tailwinds for 2026.
recent_trades.reasoning: JPM leads financials with +0.59 sentiment, +0.6% gain and no current holding; complements the recent GS initiation while staying well below single-name limits and within remaining trade budget.

recent_trades.summary: Initiating 4% PM position on strong positive sentiment and headlines framing the recent pullback as a buying opportunity for long-term investors.
recent_trades.reasoning: PM shows +0.48 sentiment, +0.6% intraday gain, attractive valuation and 5%+ yield; not currently held, fits diversification away from big-tech concentration while aligning with defensive consumer-staples strength.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming AVGO from 11.7% to 9% on negative sentiment and mixed AI earnings reaction to release capital for higher-conviction ideas.
recent_trades.reasoning: AVGO has the largest portfolio weight and carries -0.12 sentiment with headlines highlighting margin pressure at peers; recent purchase to 12% target has been achieved and a modest trim keeps exposure while staying under the 20% cap.

recent_trades.summary: Adding to NextEra Energy on positive sentiment and growing data center opportunity post-Dominion acquisition announcement.
recent_trades.reasoning: NEE’s +0.38 sentiment reflects optimism around its Dominion Energy merger and data center expansion. Despite a -1.01% unrealized loss, the technical support near the 30-day low and catalyst-rich news flow justify averaging down to a 3.5% weight.

recent_trades.summary: Adding to Deere on high positive sentiment and a recent sustainability award, raising weight to 4%.
recent_trades.reasoning: DE holds a +0.45 sentiment score with news of the John Deere Sustainability Award and positive analyst notes. The stock is up +4.61% and shows strong momentum near its 30-day high; increasing the position capitalizes on continued outperformance.

recent_trades.summary: Initiating a 3% position in Philip Morris on very positive sentiment and a pullback gift for long-term investors, confidence 8/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: PM displays a +0.48 aggregate sentiment score, the strongest among our universe, with a 5-day gain of +0.42% and Seeking Alpha calling the pullback a gift. As a defensive consumer staple with high yield, it aligns with our rotation into safer assets amid geopolitical risk.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting Walmart entirely on a -3.13% loss and absence of positive news, reallocating to higher-momentum names.
recent_trades.reasoning: Walmart has drifted lower without a catalyst, and its defensive appeal is overshadowed by stronger consumer staples like PM. Selling eliminates a drag and provides cash for immediate deployment into PM and DE.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming Disney to 2% due to lack of positive catalysts and underperformance, rotating capital into stronger names.
recent_trades.reasoning: DIS holds a 3.97% weight with a -0.88% unrealized loss and no significant positive news today. Reducing exposure frees cash for high-conviction defensive plays, while maintaining a smaller position for potential Spider-Man box office tailwinds.

recent_trades.summary: Increasing Eli Lilly allocation to 18% as the reopening of Zepbound access for 10 million US patients serves as a massive near-term revenue catalyst.
recent_trades.reasoning: News that Eli Lilly is reopening Zepbound access for 10 million patients directly impacts near-term revenue projections and solidifies its dominance in the obesity care market. A concurrent $4B bet in the vaccine market also provides excellent long-term pipeline diversification.

recent_trades.summary: Initiating an 8% position in Adobe following a major public endorsement from Michael Burry highlighting its strong AI integration and Firefly platform.
recent_trades.reasoning: Adobe is showing robust 30-day relative strength (+4.05%) and has just received a major bullish catalyst with Michael Burry calling it a 'fat pitch' due to its AI tie-ups. This high-profile backing, combined with solid technical momentum, warrants a new long position.

recent_trades.summary: Liquidating Exxon Mobil entirely to eliminate downside risk ahead of an imminent decision on the Iran deal, which could flood the market with oil supply.
recent_trades.reasoning: Macro news indicates an imminent decision by the Trump administration regarding an Iran deal. If successful, this could lift sanctions and reintroduce Iranian oil to the market, significantly depressing crude prices. Exiting XOM protects the portfolio from this binary commodity risk.

recent_trades.summary: Reducing Eli Lilly slightly as strong headlines are still not being confirmed by price momentum, confidence 7/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: LLY news sentiment is positive, but the stock remains down 1.92% over 30 days and is trading very close to its 30-day low, which argues against giving it a larger slot in an intraday momentum-oriented portfolio. Since we already cut the position earlier, I am only trimming from 3.00% to 2.00% instead of exiting outright. That keeps some exposure to the favorable obesity-access story while respecting weak technical confirmation.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming Intel again because it remains one of the weakest charts in the portfolio despite mildly improved headlines, confidence 8/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: INTC is down 3.38% over 30 days and sits just above its 30-day low, making it one of the clearest relative laggards in the current book. The recent positive headlines have not translated into sustained price strength, and I have already been reducing exposure for that reason. This is a final trim to a small residual weight rather than a full exit in case the stock stabilizes late-day.

recent_trades.summary: Adding slightly to Amazon on positive AI-related news with the stock holding near the low end of its recent range rather than breaking down, confidence 6/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: AMZN sentiment is the strongest among current holdings today, and while price action is not a breakout, it is stabilizing near the 30-day low after a shallow pullback. This offers a reasonable risk-reward add versus chasing the stocks already at highs. I am only increasing from 4.94% to 6% because momentum remains mixed and the tape is not broadly bullish.

recent_trades.summary: Adding Deere as it pushes toward a fresh 30-day high with strong positive sentiment and a steady industrial uptrend, confidence 7/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: DE is up 1.08% over 30 days and trading just below its 30-day high of 546.34, with the intraday chart showing persistent bid support rather than exhaustion. News flow is notably positive, and the current 5.06% position leaves room to scale into a stronger industrial name without concentration risk. A move to 7% is a modest add rather than an aggressive chase.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming HON from ~10% to 8% target on negative sentiment despite today's gain, confidence 6/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: HON carries -0.13 sentiment with headlines on quantum computing and Dow movers that lack conviction compared to stronger RTX/GE momentum; reducing high-weight position helps fund the PM addition and maintains portfolio under 20% single-name cap.

recent_trades.summary: Adding to NEM given strong +2.75% 30-day performance, +2.7% today, with gold continuing to hold near highs and NEM showing relative strength in the materials sector.
recent_trades.reasoning: NEM is the top performer in today's universe at +2.7%, and has the strongest 30-day return at +2.75%. Gold (GLD +0.67% 30D) remains supported as a safe haven amid negative market sentiment. NEM is currently only 0.87% of portfolio which is undweighted for its performance profile. Increasing to 5% provides meaningful exposure to the gold mining theme.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting PM as the position is slightly negative, -0.57% over 5 days, and with very limited weight (3.91%) it adds little diversification value.
recent_trades.reasoning: PM is down -0.57% over 5 days and essentially flat since purchase (-0.18% unrealized). The stock is near 30-day lows and sector rotation away from consumer staples with negative broader sentiment makes this a weak hold. The freed capital can be redeployed into higher-conviction names or held as cash buffer.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting the small DIS position as the 0.34% weight is too small to be meaningful and the position is slightly negative with no new catalysts.
recent_trades.reasoning: DIS was initiated at 0.34% weight which is negligible and provides minimal portfolio impact. The stock is -0.1% today and -0.69% over 5 days. The Spider-Man catalyst that drove the initial buy has already been priced in. Freeing up this capital allows better deployment elsewhere or just reduces noise in the portfolio.

recent_trades.summary: Increasing AVGO to 12% target on positive headlines naming it a top Steve Cohen pick, safe long-term AI stock, and positioned for next infrastructure shift.
recent_trades.reasoning: Current 7.9% weight with +1.25% unrealized gain and resilient technicals in the 30D range; news drivers not yet fully priced in versus NVDA weakness. Boosts conviction AI exposure within single-name limits as part of rotation from negative-sentiment names.

recent_trades.summary: Partially trimming CRM on lukewarm Salesforce outlook news fueling disruption fears despite today's +3.5% move.
recent_trades.reasoning: Recent initiation at ~4% target now warrants profit-taking given the May 28 outlook headline not fully reflected in technical rebound; keeps partial exposure aligned with AI theme but frees capital. Avoids full exit to respect recent positive momentum.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming NVDA position on negative sentiment, flat trading despite high expectations, and Cramer commentary preferring an Apple-like approach.
recent_trades.reasoning: Current 6% weight with near-zero unrealized P/L; headlines indicate expectations are already priced in with risk of disappointment. Reducing exposure while retaining core position; proceeds help fund AVGO increase without net cash use.

recent_trades.summary: Increasing Broadcom allocation to 15% on highly positive news sentiment and strong institutional backing for its AI infrastructure positioning.
recent_trades.reasoning: Broadcom is experiencing highly positive news flow, repeatedly highlighted as a top long-term pick for the next major shift in AI infrastructure. Increasing our weight captures this momentum and capitalizes on its relative strength in a mixed market environment.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming NVIDIA exposure from 14% to 10% to manage risk amid negative news sentiment and sluggish price action.
recent_trades.reasoning: Despite massive long-term sales targets, NVIDIA's price action remains flat, and recent headlines highlight potential macroeconomic debt concerns. Trimming the position allows us to de-risk slightly while maintaining a core allocation to the leading AI chipmaker.

recent_trades.summary: Liquidating Salesforce entirely following market reports of a lukewarm outlook that has fueled disruption fears.
recent_trades.reasoning: Recent headlines indicate a disappointing fundamental outlook for Salesforce, which invalidates our previous short-term technical breakout thesis. Exiting the position entirely protects the portfolio from further downside and frees up capital.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming CRM from 5% to 2% on Salesforce lukewarm outlook headline fueling disruption fears — a material negative catalyst since my buy earlier today.
recent_trades.reasoning: The Yahoo Finance headline 'Salesforce Gives Lukewarm Outlook That Fuels Disruption Fear' is a significant fundamental negative that emerged after my CRM entry at 5%. The stock is already showing -0.33% unrealized loss. Reducing exposure but keeping a small position in case the selloff is overdone.

recent_trades.summary: Adding slightly to Oracle as it continues to press fresh 30-day highs with one of the cleanest uptrends in the universe, confidence 6/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: ORCL is the strongest tech chart on the board today, up 2.65% over 30 days and trading essentially at its 30-day high with steady intraday follow-through. News sentiment is mildly negative, so I am only adding modestly rather than aggressively, but relative strength versus semis and mega-cap internet still supports a small increase.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming Intel again because it is still one of the weakest trend structures in the book despite today’s small bounce, confidence 8/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: INTC has the weakest 30-day return among current holdings shown, is much closer to its 30-day low than high, and the intraday chart shows only a minor stabilization after persistent pressure. Since we still hold shares after prior trims and the chart has not meaningfully improved, reducing from 3.00% to 2.00% is a disciplined continuation of de-risking.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming AMD as it remains below its 30-day high and intraday momentum is weaker than the stronger software and industrial leaders, confidence 7/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: AMD is down over the past 5 and 30 days, sits just above its 30-day low, and the intraday candle structure remains soft after a sharp early drop. We already reduced it earlier, but the current 5.96% weight is still sizable relative to its cooling momentum, so cutting toward 4% lowers risk without fully exiting a profitable position.

recent_trades.summary: Adding significantly to LLY as Zepbound access reopens for 10 million US patients - a major new fundamental catalyst driving the stock to all-time highs today.
recent_trades.reasoning: LLY is up +4% today to all-time highs on the Zepbound reopen news for 10 million US patients - this is a genuine demand inflection point that wasn't priced in at my last purchase. The stock is currently at ~$1,093 with strong positive momentum. Adding from ~11.7% to ~17.5% weight given the fundamental significance of the Zepbound access expansion, vaccine market entry ($4B bet), and ADA Scientific event upcoming. Sentiment is +0.11 but the specific Zepbound headline is a major bullish driver.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting AMD as it's down -2% today with neutral sentiment, near 30-day lows, and the Zepbound catalyst provides better capital deployment in LLY.
recent_trades.reasoning: AMD is down -2% on the session with neutral sentiment (0.00), showing weakness near 30-day lows (~$507-510 range). The analyst target of $167 from recent news suggests significant upside but near-term momentum is negative. Selling the full ~5% position to fund LLY addition following the major Zepbound reopen catalyst which is a genuine new fundamental driver.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming AMD from 3.75% to 2% — weakest tech performer today at -2%, trading near 30-day lows with no positive catalyst to reverse the slide.
recent_trades.reasoning: AMD is down 2% today and -2% over 30 days, consistently underperforming the tech group. Neutral sentiment and price near 30-day lows at $510 vs $507 support. Reducing exposure to redeploy capital to stronger names.

recent_trades.summary: Liquidating our Intel position entirely to cut losses as the stock continues to face downward pressure and technical weakness.
recent_trades.reasoning: Intel is down nearly 3% today and continues to test 30-day lows, indicating a clear bearish trend. We are closing out this position to free up capital for stronger momentum plays like CRM rather than tying up funds in an underperformer.

recent_trades.summary: Initiating a 6% position in Salesforce to capture its technical breakout and strong intraday momentum.
recent_trades.reasoning: CRM is breaking out with a +3.9% gain today and solid momentum over the last 30 days. Positive sentiment and media mentions support this upward trajectory, so we are allocating 6% to capitalize on the ongoing rally.

recent_trades.summary: Adding slightly to ORCL as it presses fresh highs and remains one of the cleaner AI infrastructure trend setups, confidence 7/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: ORCL has gained 1.9% over 30 days and is trading at the top of its recent range, with the intraday candles continuing to hold the breakout area. We initiated it recently, but price action has meaningfully improved since then, and a modest step-up from 3.3% to 5% reflects improving trend quality without overconcentrating.

recent_trades.summary: Adding modestly to CRM on continued leadership near a 30-day high with supportive enterprise AI sentiment, confidence 8/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: CRM is up 3.9% over 30 days and is trading right near its 30-day high, with the intraday chart showing steady upside follow-through rather than a failed breakout. Positive AI and enterprise software headlines support the trend, and increasing from 4.7% to 6% keeps the size disciplined while leaning into one of the strongest names in the portfolio.

recent_trades.summary: Cutting LLY further as positive obesity-access headlines are not yet translating into price strength, confidence 7/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: The news flow is supportive, but LLY is still down 1.9% over 30 days and sitting just above its 30-day low, indicating the market is not rewarding the catalyst yet. Since we already trimmed once and the stock continues to lag while capital is scarce, reducing to a smaller placeholder position is prudent.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming INTC again as it remains one of the weakest charts in the portfolio despite mildly positive AI-related headlines, confidence 8/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: INTC is down 2.9% over 30 days and trading very close to its 30-day low, with intraday candles still showing weak follow-through relative to stronger tech peers. We already reduced it earlier, and the technical picture has not improved enough to justify a 5% weight while better momentum opportunities exist elsewhere.

recent_trades.summary: Initiating 4% GS position on positive sentiment from expected record 2026 M&A activity and +1.3% intraday gain, confidence 7/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: Financials sector showing relative strength; GS technical chart displays clear uptrend with higher highs; diversifies the tech-heavy portfolio while staying under 20% single-name cap and using capital from AMD exit.

universe.description: 79-name universe: 75 top U.S. mega/large-cap stocks with full GICS sector coverage plus 4 commodity ETFs (gold, silver, oil, copper).

claude._pin_status: UNPINNED: Anthropic echoes the alias unchanged; current dated snapshot id unconfirmed. Resolve manually before pinning (a wrong id silently changes the running model).

gemini._pin_status: UNPINNED: Google echoes the alias unchanged. The adapter now captures response.model_version, so the real dated id will surface in model_id_returned on the next live run; pin from that.

deepseek._pin_note: Deliberately repointed deepseek-v4-flash -> deepseek-v4-pro on 2026-05-21: the deepseek-reasoner alias deprecates 2026-07-24 and maps to sub-frontier v4-flash; v4-pro is DeepSeek's current frontier reasoning tier, restoring cohort parity. No dated immutable snapshot exists beneath deepseek-v4-pro, so this is the most specific callable id (same standard as the dateless Claude ids); residual drift risk if DeepSeek repoints it. Runs in thinking mode at reasoning_effort=high (see deepseek_adapter.py).

market_brief.brief: Welcome. U.S. equities: S&P +0.16%, Nasdaq +0.11%, Dow +0.54%. Technology led sectors (+2.75%) while Consumer Staples lagged (-1.90%). GPT-5.4 led the day at +1.14%. 6 of 6 models green on the day. Cumulative leader: DeepSeek V4 Pro at +22.79%. Key headline: Salesforce Gives Lukewarm Outlook That Fuels Disruption Fear - Yahoo Finance — headwind for risk assets.
market_brief.key_moves: CRM +9.2% (held by 3/6) | ORCL +8.9% (held by 2/6) | ADBE +6.0% (held by 2/6)
market_brief.macro_sentence: Key headline: Salesforce Gives Lukewarm Outlook That Fuels Disruption Fear - Yahoo Finance — headwind for risk assets.
